content_id: Tetapi ketika sehat ia penuh semangat dan memiliki harapan untuk bertahan lama, dan ini dapat menyebabkannya menjadi terlalu gembira dan tidak peduli, sehingga rasa takut harus lebih dominan agar aman dari hal itu. Ada juga yang berpendapat bahwa harapan dan rasa takut harus seimbang sehingga harapannya tidak menyebabkannya merasa aman dari Allah, dan rasa takutnya tidak menyebabkannya berputus asa dari rahmat Allah, karena keduanya adalah hal yang buruk dan menyebabkan kehancuran seseorang. Dalil untuk rasa hormat yang mendalam/gentar (al-khasyah) adalah firman Allah Yang Maha Tinggi: 'Karena itu janganlah kamu takut kepada mereka, tetapi takutlah kepada-Ku.' [Surat al-Ma'idah (5): 3]. Al-Khasyah adalah rasa takut yang didasarkan pada pengetahuan tentang keagungan Dzat yang ditakuti, dan Kedaulatan serta Kekuasaan yang sempurna yang dimiliki-Nya, karena Allah Yang Maha Tinggi berfirman: 'Sesungguhnya yang takut kepada Allah di antara hamba-hamba-Nya, hanyalah ulama.' [Surat Fathir (35): 28]. Artinya mereka yang mengetahui tentang keagungan-Nya dan Kedaulatan-Nya yang sempurna. Ini adalah sesuatu yang lebih khusus daripada rasa takut biasa, dan perbedaan antara keduanya akan menjadi jelas dengan memberikan contoh: Jika kamu takut kepada seseorang, tetapi kamu tidak tahu apakah ia mampu menangkapmu atau tidak, maka ini adalah rasa takut (khawf). Tetapi ketika kamu takut kepada seseorang dan tahu bahwa ia sepenuhnya mampu menguasaimu maka itulah rasa takut yang mendalam (khasyah). Berkenaan dengan hukum-hukum untuk setiap jenis rasa takut ini, maka sama dengan apa yang telah disebutkan sebelumnya berkenaan dengan rasa takut yang penuh hormat (khawf). Dalil untuk kembali bertobat (al-inabah) adalah firman Allah Yang Maha Tinggi: 'Dan kembalilah kamu kepada Tuhanmu, dan berserah dirilah kepada-Nya.' [Surat az-Zumar (39): 54]. Al-Inabah adalah kembali kepada Allah Yang Maha Tinggi dalam ketaatan, dan menghindari kemaksiatan kepada-Nya, dan maknanya dekat dengan taubat, kecuali bahwa ia lebih halus maknanya, dan menyampaikan rasa ketergantungan kepada Allah dan melarikan diri untuk berlindung kepada-Nya, dan tidak bisa selain untuk Allah Yang Maha Tinggi. Dalilnya adalah firman Allah Yang Maha Tinggi: 'Dan kembalilah kamu kepada Tuhanmu, dan berserah dirilah kepada-Nya.' [Surat az-Zumar (39): 54]. Yang dimaksud dengan firman Allah Yang Maha Tinggi '...berserah dirilah kepada-Nya' adalah: Islam sebagaimana yang ditetapkan dan diwajibkan atas kita dalam Syariat yaitu tunduk kepada hukum-hukum dan perintah-perintah Allah. Hal ini karena penyerahan diri kepada Allah Yang Maha Tinggi ada dua jenis: (i) Penyerahan seluruh ciptaan, yaitu tunduk kepada apa pun yang Dia tetapkan harus ada dan terjadi dalam ciptaan yang tidak ada pilihan bagi ciptaan atasnya. Ini bersifat umum dan mencakup semua orang di langit dan di bumi, orang-orang beriman dan orang-orang kafir, orang-orang saleh dan orang-orang durhaka. Tidak ada seorang pun yang mungkin meremehkan dan menyimpang dari ini. Dalilnya adalah firman Allah Yang Maha Tinggi: 'Dan kepada-Nya-lah siapa pun yang di langit dan di bumi berserah diri, baik suka maupun terpaksa, dan hanya kepada Allahlah mereka dikembalikan.' [Surat Ali Imran (3): 83]. (ii) Penyerahan kepada hukum-hukum dan ketentuan-ketentuan Islam, dan ini adalah tunduk dalam ketaatan. Ini khusus bagi mereka yang taat kepada Allah, para rasul dan mereka yang mengikuti mereka dengan kebaikan. Dalil-dalil untuk ini dalam Al-Qur'an sangat banyak, dan di antaranya adalah ayah yang disebutkan oleh penulis [Surat az-Zumar (39): 54]. Dalil untuk memohon bantuan dan pertolongan (al-isti'anah) adalah firman Allah Yang Maha Tinggi: 'Hanya Engkaulah yang kami sembah, dan hanya kepada Engkaulah kami meminta pertolongan.' [Surat al-Fatihah (1): 5]. Dan dalam hadis disebutkan: 'Jika kamu meminta pertolongan, maka mintalah pertolongan kepada Allah.' [Diriwayatkan oleh at-Tirmidzi dan dinyatakan sahih oleh Syaikh al-Albani dalam al-Misykat (no. 5302)]. Al-Isti'anah adalah memohon bantuan, dan ada berbagai jenisnya: (i) Memohon bantuan dan pertolongan kepada Allah - ini adalah permohonan bantuan dan pertolongan yang mencakup kerendahan hati yang sempurna dari hamba di hadapan Tuhannya, dan menyerahkan serta mempercayakan urusan kepada-Nya, dan yakin bahwa Dia sepenuhnya mencukupi baginya. Ini tidak boleh kecuali untuk Allah Yang Maha Tinggi semata, dan dalilnya adalah firman Allah Yang Maha Tinggi: 'Hanya Engkaulah yang kami sembah, dan hanya kepada Engkaulah kami meminta pertolongan.' [Surat al-Fatihah (1): 5]. Bahwa ini hanya untuk Allah Yang Maha Tinggi semata ditunjukkan oleh fakta bahwa Dia menyebutkan kata yang menjadi objek tindakan sebelum kata kerja itu sendiri, dan menurut kaidah tata bahasa Arab, bahwa Al-Qur'an diturunkan dengannya, mendahulukan apa yang biasanya datang belakangan menunjukkan pembatasan dan pengkhususan. Sehingga mengarahkan jenis ini kepada selain Allah Yang Maha Tinggi adalah syirik yang mengeluarkan seseorang dari agama. (ii) Meminta bantuan dari seseorang dari kalangan makhluk, dalam sesuatu yang ia mampu membantunya. Maka ini akan sesuai dengan tindakan yang dimintakan bantuannya.
content_en: But when healthy he is energetic and has hope of remaining for a long time, and this may lead him to be exuberant and carefree, so fear should predominate in order to be safe from that. It is also said that the earnest hope and fear should be equal so that his hope does not lead him to feel safe from Allaah, and his fear does not cause him to despair of Allaah's mercy, since both of these are evil and cause a person's destruction. The evidence for awe/dread (al-khashyah) is the Saying of Allaah, the Most High: "So do not have awe of them, but have awe of Me” [77] [surah al-Maa'idah (5):3]. ________________________________________________________ Awe {al-khashyah) is fear founded upon knowledge of the greatness of the One whom he fears, and the complete and perfect Sovereignty and Dominion that is His, since Allaah, the Most High, says, "Only those who have knowledge will truly have awe of Allaah." [surah Faatir (35):28] Meaning those who know of His greatness and perfect Sovereignty. It is something more particular than fear, and the difference between it and fear will become clear by giving an example: If you fear someone, but you do not know whether he is able to catch you or not, then this is fear (khawf). But when you fear someone and know that he is fully able to seize hold of you then that is fear/dread (khashyah). With regard to speech about the rulings for each class of fear/dread, then it is the same as for what has already been said with regard to reverential fear (khawf). And the evidence for turning repentantly (al-inaabah) is the Saying of Allaah, the Most High: "So turn, O you people, repentantly and obediently to your Lord, and submit obediently to Him" [78] [surah az-Zumar (39):54]. ________________________________________________________ Al-Inaabah is to turn to Allaah, the Most High, in obedience, and avoiding disobedience to Him, and its meaning is close to that of repentance (tawbah), except that it is more subtle in meaning, and conveys the sense of dependance upon Allaah and fleeing for refuge to Him, and it cannot be except for Allaah, the Most High. Its evidence is the Saying of Allaah, the Most High, "So turn, O you people, repentantly and obediently to your Lord, and submit obediently to Him" [surah az-Zumar (39):54] What is meant by the Saying of Allaah, the Most High, "...submit obediently to Him" is: Islaam as prescribed and required of us in the Sharee'ah and that is to submit to the laws and commandments of Allaah. This is because submission to Allaah, the Most High, is of two types: (i) Submission of all the creation, which is to submit to whatever He has decreed should exist and occur in the creation about which the creation has no choice. So this is general and covers everyone in the heavens and the earth, the Believers and the unbelievers, the righteous and the wicked. No one can possibly disdain and depart from this. The evidence for it is the Saying of Allaah, the Most High, "And to Him everyone in the heavens and the earth submit, willingly or unwillingly, and to Him you will all be returned." [surah Aal-'Imraan (3):83] (ii) Submission to the laws and prescriptions of Islaam, and this is to submit in obedience. This is particular to those who are obedient to Allaah, the Messengers and those who follow them upon good. The evidences for this in the Qur'aan are many, and from them is the Aayah mentioned by the author [surah az-Zumar (39): 5] The evidence for appealing for aid and assistance (al-isti'aanah) is the Saying of Allaah, the Most High: "O Allaah You alone we worship, and to You alone we appeal for aid" [surah al-Faatihah (1):5] and in the hadith there occurs: "If you seek help, then seek the help of Allaah.” [79] [Reported by at-Tirmidhee and declared sahih by Shaykh al-Albaanee in al-Mishkaat (no.5302)] ________________________________________________________ Al-istVaanah is to appeal for assistance, and it is of various types: (i) Appealing for aid and assistance from Allaah - this is an appeal for aid and assistance that comprises complete humility of the servant before his Lord, and to submit and entrust the affair to Him, and to be certain that He is fully sufficient for him. This is not to be except for Allaah, the Most High, alone, and the evidence is the Saying of Allaah, the Most High, "O Allaah You alone we worship, and to You alone we appeal for aid." [surah al-Faatihah (1):5] The fact that this is to be for Allaah, the Most High, alone is shown by the fact that He mentions the word upon which the verb acts before the verb itself, and according to the principles of grammar of the Arabic language, in which the Qur'aan came down, bringing forward that which usually comes afterwards indicates restriction and particularisation. So directing this type to other than Allaah, the Most High, is shirk which takes a person out of the Religion. (ii) Seeking the help of a person from the creation, in something which he is capable of helping in. Then this will be in accordance with the action in which help is sought.
content_ar: But عندما healthy he is energetic and has الأمل of remaining for a long time, and this may lead him to be exuberant and carefree, لذا الخوف should predominate من أجل be safe from that. It is أيضاً said that the earnest الأمل and الخوف should be equal لذا that his الأمل does not lead him to feel safe from Allaah, and his الخوف does not cause him to despair of Allaah's الرحمة, بما أن both of these are الشر and cause a person's destruction. The evidence for awe/dread (al-khashyah) is the Saying of Allaah, the Most High: "لذا do not have awe of them, but have awe of Me” [77] [السورة al-Maa'idah (5):3]. ________________________________________________________ Awe {al-khashyah) is الخوف founded upon العلم of the greatness of the One whom he fears, and the complete and perfect Sovereignty and Dominion that is His, بما أن Allaah, the Most High, says, "Only those who have العلم will truly have awe of Allaah." [السورة Faatir (35):28] Meaning those who know of His greatness and perfect Sovereignty. It is something more particular than الخوف, and the difference between it and الخوف will become clear by giving an example: إذا you الخوف someone, but you do not know whether he is able to catch you or not, then this is الخوف (khawf). But عندما you الخوف someone and know that he is fully able to seize hold of you then that is الخوف/dread (khashyah). فيما يتعلق بـ speech about the rulings for each class of الخوف/dread, then it is the same أما what has already been said فيما يتعلق بـ reverential الخوف (khawf). And the evidence for turning repentantly (al-inaabah) is the Saying of Allaah, the Most High: "لذا turn, O you people, repentantly and obediently to your Lord, and submit obediently to Him" [78] [السورة az-Zumar (39):54]. ________________________________________________________ Al-Inaabah is to turn to Allaah, the Most High, in obedience, and avoiding disobedience to Him, and its meaning is close to that of التوبة (التوبة), except that it is more subtle in meaning, and conveys the sense of dependance upon Allaah and fleeing for refuge to Him, and it cannot be except for Allaah, the Most High. Its evidence is the Saying of Allaah, the Most High, "لذا turn, O you people, repentantly and obediently to your Lord, and submit obediently to Him" [السورة az-Zumar (39):54] What is meant by the Saying of Allaah, the Most High, "...submit obediently to Him" is: Islaam as prescribed and required of us in the Sharee'ah and that is to submit to the laws and commandments of Allaah. This is لأن submission to Allaah, the Most High, is of two types: (i) Submission of all the creation, which is to submit to whatever He has decreed should exist and occur in the creation about which the creation has no choice. لذا this is general and covers everyone in the heavens and the earth, the المؤمنون and the unbelievers, the الصالح and the wicked.
